title: stack-provision policy
description: Policy file consumed by skills/stack-provision/scripts/orchestrate.mjs to drive the single-entry stack-provision UX. Lives at .omc/stack-provision-policy.json by default. Hand-edited rarely; the orchestrator and the omc-stack CLI maintain reasonable defaults so most users never see this file.

auto_approve_threshold.description: Source-trust score at or above which a candidate is auto-approved when no warning/critical risk flags are present and the strict gate passes. Independent of the strict gate floor (0.85).

blocked_sources.description: Sources whose candidates are auto-rejected even if they would otherwise pass. Use to opt out of a registry the project does not trust.

forbid_generated_drafts.description: When true (default), LLM-generated draft skills can never be auto-approved. They must be explicitly edited before approval (Phase 2 wires the editor flow).

headless_action.description: What the orchestrator does when manual decisions are needed but no TTY/stdin is available. bail: emit completed:cancelled and exit. auto_approve_safe: approve every candidate that passes the strict gate AND has no warning/critical flags AND is not generated. manifest_only: write the install plan but skip promote/verify.

approved_by.description: Identity recorded in review-decision.json when the orchestrator drives review. Override per-CI-job for audit clarity.

critic_verdict.description: Critic verdict forwarded to provision.mjs review. Only approve permits promote. Revise/rewind are accepted to model a CI gate that explicitly rejects.

skill_root.description: Absolute or project-relative path where promoted skills are installed. null defaults to .claude/skills/omc-provisioned under the project root.

scan_global_skill_roots.description: When true, the orchestrator scans well-known global skill roots (~/.claude/skills, ~/.config/claude/skills, ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/skills, plus the project .claude/skills) during classify and flags candidates whose slug already exists elsewhere. Duplicates are routed to manual review with a duplicate-already-installed flag; sha-mismatch lifts severity. Default false because global scans cross project boundaries — opt in per project.

max_decisions_per_session.description: Soft ceiling on the number of manual decisions the interactive flow asks in one run. Beyond this the orchestrator emits a batch_resolve summary and asks the user to approve a remaining batch in one go.

cve_check.description: Phase 4: query OSV.dev for known advisories on each candidate's declared npm/cargo/PyPI package and add cve:<severity>:<id> risk_flags. Network failures degrade silently — offline runs behave as before.

revalidation_enabled.description: Phase 4: opportunistic weekly revalidation of installed skills (re-hash on disk + best-effort upstream check). Runs at most once per `revalidation_interval_days` per project. Disable for headless CI runs that should never touch the network.

revalidation_interval_days.description: Phase 4: minimum days between opportunistic revalidations. State persists in .omc/stack-provision/revalidation.json.

cleanup_enabled.description: Phase 4: emit a cleanup_proposal event listing skills idle for more than `cleanup_threshold_days`. The orchestrator never deletes silently — the operator must confirm.

cleanup_threshold_days.description: Phase 4: skills not used for at least this many days are surfaced as deprecation candidates. State persists in .omc/stack-provision/usage.json.

offline.description: Phase 4: when true, every network-touching feature (CVE feed, upstream revalidation) is bypassed and reports degraded results. Useful for air-gapped CI.

tofu_enabled.description: Phase 5: trust-on-first-use pinning. The first successful install snapshots the SKILL.md content under .omc/stack-provision/pins/<slug>.snapshot.md and records its sha256 in pins.json. Subsequent revisits with a different sha256 are routed to manual review with a unified diff.

tofu_strict.description: Phase 5: when true, any TOFU drift (pinned sha != current sha) becomes a critical risk_flag (rejected). When false (default), drift is a warn-class flag that requires manual approval.

sandbox_dryrun_enabled.description: Phase 5: static behavior fingerprint of each candidate's SKILL.md — detects undeclared tool callouts, dangerous shell commands (curl|sh, rm -rf /, --dangerously-skip-permissions), sensitive env reads. critical-severity findings force manual review.

sandbox_max_tools.description: Phase 5: optional per-skill maximum on the number of distinct tools the sandbox fingerprint may detect. Skills exceeding the limit get a warn-class risk_flag. null disables the count gate.
